briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was discovered to be run by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the fintech middleware model.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of Credit Kudos signals growing Big Tech interest in leveraging open banking data for financial services.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, challenging traditional banking models.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Feds reveal streams behind $1-billion VC initiative as budget becomes law](https://betakit.com/feds-reveal-streams-behind-1-billion-vc-initiative-as-budget-becomes-law/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government has disclosed the funding streams underpinning its $1-billion venture capital initiative as the enabling budget legislation receives royal assent, with potential implications for fintech startups across Canada.

- **[Canadian VCs are starting to fear an AI-driven "SaaSpocalypse"](https://betakit.com/canadian-vcs-are-starting-to-fear-an-ai-driven-saaspocalypse/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian venture capitalists are growing concerned that AI-driven automation could disrupt or destroy the SaaS business models underpinning much of the country's tech and fintech investment portfolios.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's push to move beyond screen scraping toward a formal open banking framework remains a critical backdrop for understanding the country's ongoing fintech regulatory evolution.

- **[Settlement approved for Canadians affected by past 23andMe data breach](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/23andme-settlement-class-action-9.7145473?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — A class action settlement has been approved for Canadian consumers impacted by the 23andMe data breach, raising continued concerns about data privacy in consumer-facing tech and fintech platforms.

- **[Feds put nearly $29 million toward cleantech projects across Canada](https://betakit.com/feds-put-nearly-29-million-toward-cleantech-projects-across-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government is directing nearly $29 million to cleantech initiatives nationwide, reflecting broader public investment trends relevant to impact-focused fintech and sustainable finance ecosystems in Canada.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Feds reveal streams behind $1-billion VC initiative as budget becomes law](https://betakit.com/feds-reveal-streams-behind-1-billion-vc-initiative-as-budget-becomes-law/)** — *BetaKit* — Atlantic Canadian fintech startups and ecosystem builders should pay close attention as the federal government details how its $1-billion VC initiative will be structured, potentially opening new funding pathways for early-stage companies in the region.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A cautionary tale for fintech founders and investors everywhere, this case underscores the importance of transparency, governance, and authentic innovation — values central to building a trustworthy Atlantic fintech ecosystem.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — As Atlantic Canada's credit unions and fintech startups look toward Canada's evolving open banking framework, this analysis of how open banking drives payments competition offers valuable strategic context for regional ecosystem players.
